claim: Twelfth MCC measurement: v10b composed detector with the 7 additional annotation-class keyword rules (DNA topoisomerase, lipid biosynthesis, tRNA modification, molecular chaperone, PTS transporter, nucleotide-salvage kinase, nucleotide biosynthesis), each validated to hit 0 false positives on the Breuer Nonessential set. Full 455-gene sweep at seed=42: MCC = 0.364 on the imbalanced Breuer set (Quasiessential = positive). This is the same stack the balanced-40 panel scored MCC 0.70 on.
source_detail: Reproducible via 'python scripts/run_sweep_parallel.py --all --workers 4 --use-rust --scale 0.05 --t-end-s 0.5 --detector composed --seed 42'. Preserved outputs at outputs/predictions_*_all455_v10b.csv + outputs/metrics_*_all455_v10b.json.
caveats: Same stack scores MCC 0.70 on the standard balanced-40 panel. Full-set MCC is depressed by the 5.3:1 positive:negative imbalance combined with Quasiessential treated as positive.
Precision 0.974 means when we call a gene essential we're right 26 out of 27 times. The 6 FPs are all carry-over from v5/v9 — known iMB155 metabolic-reconstruction gaps. Unaffected by detector work.

Recall 0.582 means we catch ~58% of true positives. The remaining ~42% comprise: uncharacterized proteins (no annotation signal), metabolic enzymes outside the 356-reaction iMB155 SBML, regulatory proteins without complex membership, and tRNA modification enzymes without matching keywords.
Runtime: 941s for 455 genes at 4 workers on a 4-core box (2.1s/gene effective wall). Priors-only scan of the same 455 genes: ~200ms.
All data is bundled in the repo. Zero network, zero API, zero per-run cost.
